data.to: Hepatology Department, Milton Keynes University Hospital
data.from: Dr. R. Kapoor, Bletchley Medical Centre
data.reason_for_referral: Painless progressive jaundice with significant weight loss (10kg over 2 months), pale stools, and dark urine. Urgent evaluation for possible biliary obstruction or pancreatic malignancy.
data.alcohol_intake: Social only, approximately 5 units per week.

data.chief_complaint: Noticed yellowing of skin and eyes over past 3 weeks. Wife noted it first. Reports 10kg unintentional weight loss over 2 months. Stools pale and greasy. Urine dark brown.

physical_examination.abdomen: Soft, non-tender. No palpable masses. Liver edge just palpable.
physical_examination.skin: Marked jaundice of sclera and skin. No spider naevi.

data.assessment: Clinical picture concerning for obstructive jaundice — needs urgent imaging to exclude pancreatic head lesion.

sections.title: Pre-Consultation Questionnaire
qa: Yellow skin/eyes, weight loss, pale stools, dark urine. No pain.
About 5 units per week. Beer at weekends.
Type 2 diabetes. No high blood pressure.
